title: Smoky Gooey Peruvian Haddock Stir-fry
description: A quick-fix peruvian dish featuring smoked haddock, blanching it in a gooey sauce for a smoky flavor. Suitable for Jewish dietary restrictions (Kosher).

ingredients.name: Red Peppers
ingredients.notes: Diced into thin strips, remove seeds for milder flavor

ingredients.name: Tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Roasted under oven to soften and concentrate the flavors

ingredients.name: Cumin Seeds
ingredients.notes: Preferably roasted before grinding for extra depth of flavor

ingredients.name: Coconut Milk
ingredients.notes: Use full-fat to achieve gooey texture

instructions: Preheat your wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Season the haddock with salt and let it sit for about a minute on each side.
In the same pan, toast cumin seeds until fragrant, around 30 seconds to 1 minute. Add garlic cloves and onion, stir-fry until translucent, adding minced peppers after one minute.
Add tomatoes along with their juices, coconut milk, salt, and pepper to taste, bring to a boil then reduce heat to low-medium and let it simmer for about 15 minutes. Stir occasionally.
Toss in the haddock fillets directly from their packaging into the sauce, allowing them to cook on both sides until golden brown and crispy. Serve hot.
